name: Larry Darnell
abstract: Larry Darnell (born Leo Edward Donald, Jr.; December 17, 1928, Columbus, Ohio – July 3, 1983, Columbus) was a successful American singer, who was instrumental in the formation of the New Orleans style of R&B in the late 1940s and early 1950s.

about_quotes.speaker: George "Tex" Stephens
about_quotes.evidence: Then Larry Darnell came to town. I don't know exactly where Larry Darnell came from.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: "Sing me a song like Larry Darnell or somebody. Let me hear, even Billy Eckstine."
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
